content:
How I fused MERN, polished React/Vite interfaces, and cloud hosting to turn a portfolio dashboard into a live trading experience.

3 min read
Just now

--

Introduction :-
StockVerse is a MERN portfolio project designed to showcase a modern stock trading experience without relying on live market feeds or authentication. I wanted a clean, deployable app that combined :-

• a React/Vite landing page for marketing and product messaging

• a React/Vite dashboard for portfolio interaction

• an Express/Node backend for holdings, positions, and orders

• a MongoDB data layer for persistent sample portfolio data
The result is a polished full-stack demo with separate deploy targets: Vercel for both frontends and Render for the backend.
👉 Try the live StockVerse app here : StockVerse
👉 GitHub Repository : StockVerse_Github_repo
What StockVerse Does :-
StockVerse delivers a complete end-to-end trading dashboard workflow using mocked portfolio data:

• Landing page sections: Home, About, Features, Pricing, Support

• Dashboard routes: Summary, Holdings, Positions, Orders

• API-backed data fetches for holdings, positions, and orders

• A simple order creation flow using POST /newOrder

• Database persistence with MongoDB and Mongoose

• CORS-enabled backend so frontends can talk to the API from different origins
This is intentionally a portfolio-style demo rather than a production trading platform. That means:

• no authentication

• no live stock API integration

• no user accounts
Demo Screens :-
🏠 Landing Page Overview
Press enter or click to view image in full size

📊 Dashboard Summary
Press enter or click to view image in full size

📈 Holdings / Orders View
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Tech Stack :-
StockVerse uses a modern web stack across three apps:

• Frontend landing: React, Vite, React Router, Bootstrap

• Dashboard frontend: React, Vite, React Router, Material UI, Chart.js, Axios

• Backend: Node.js, Express, Mongoose, MongoDB, CORS, body-parser, dotenv

• Deployment: Vercel for frontend/dashboard, Render for backend
Architecture Overview :-

• React/Vite landing frontend lives in frontend

• React/Vite dashboard frontend lives in dashboard

• Express backend lives in backend

• MongoDB storage is accessed through process.env.ATLASDB_URL

• Dashboard API calls use Axios to fetch:

• GET /allHoldings

• GET /allPositions

• GET /orders

• POST /newOrder
Why Vercel and Render :-

• Vercel is ideal for React/Vite static apps with automatic builds, preview URLs, and fast CDN performance. That’s why both the landing page and dashboard are hosted there.

• Render is a better fit for the backend because it supports long-running Express services, environment variables, and backend process hosting.
The Backend in Brief :-
The backend is a simple Express API with these key behaviors:

• connects to MongoDB with Mongoose

• exposes holdings, positions, and orders endpoints

• saves new orders via a JSON payload

• supports sample data initialization through helper routes

• uses CORS so the dashboard can consume the API across domains
Why This Project Matters :-
StockVerse is a strong portfolio piece because it demonstrates how to build and deploy a real-looking MERN product with separate frontends and backend hosting. It also shows how to keep the app clean by focusing on core functionality and avoiding unnecessary complexity like authentication or live market APIs.
Lessons Learned :-

• Deploy frontends and backend separately when they have different runtime needs

• Use Vite for fast React development and easy Vercel deployment

• Keep the backend API minimal and focused on the data the UI needs

• MongoDB + Mongoose is a good fit for storing structured portfolio/sample data

• A clean UI can make a demo app feel much more polished
Limitations & Future Work :-

• No user login or protected routes

• No real-time stock data

• No account or portfolio management per user

• Future improvements could include:

• live price feeds

• authentication and user sessions

• order history tied to user accounts

• better error handling and input validation
Final Thoughts :-
StockVerse is a compact but complete MERN portfolio demo that proves a lot can be built with React/Vite, Express, MongoDB, and modern deployment tools. It’s a great project to show on GitHub or a portfolio because it includes both customer-facing landing pages and a functional dashboard backed by a real API.
